cot_prefix: Okay, so the user wants me to analyze this ethical dilemma where a student finds out their best friend plagiarized an assignment. The student has to decide whether to report their friend or stay silent. Let me start by breaking down the different aspects of this problem. First, the key players here are the student (the one facing the dilemma), the friend (the one who plagiarized), the professor, and the institution. The main ethical principles involved are honesty, integrity, loyalty, friendship, academic dishonesty, and the consequences of each action. The student values honesty and integrity, which suggests they prioritize academic and moral standards. But they're also in a close friendship, which adds an emotional component.
